skill.name: disaster-recovery
skill.description: Implement disaster recovery and backup strategies for Proxmox. Create and manage backups, test recovery procedures, and ensure business continuity for your infrastructure.
skill.summary: Implement disaster recovery and backup strategies for Proxmox. Create and manage backups, test recov...
skill.author: DataKnifeAI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged inline markdown tool names as Ruby backtick execution and flagged the description as weak cryptography. Review found no executable code, shell invocation, cryptographic implementation, or prompt injection. The remaining risk is medium because restore and delete backup workflows can affect infrastructure data.

medium_findings.title: Operationally Destructive Recovery Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill documents restore and delete backup workflows for Proxmox. These are intended capabilities, but incorrect use can overwrite systems or remove recovery data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed tools explicitly include restore and delete backup operations. The risk is operational misuse, not malicious code execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Inline Tool Names Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Lines 42 through 50 and line 71 use markdown inline code formatting for tool names. No Ruby, shell, or command execution syntax is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain markdown documentation. There is no executable file or command construction path in the reviewed skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The line contains a plain English description of disaster recovery. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing call, or encryption implementation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static high finding maps to metadata text only. No code or crypto API usage is present at the cited location.

content.value_statement: Proxmox environments need reliable backups, restore plans, and recovery tests. This skill helps teams plan, monitor, and document disaster recovery workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides VM and container backup creation workflows.
Supports restore planning for VMs and containers.
Documents snapshot creation and snapshot recovery steps.
Helps review backup status, history, and storage usage.
Creates disaster recovery test plans and reports.
Helps define backup retention and continuity practices.
limitations: It does not replace tested Proxmox backup infrastructure.
It cannot guarantee backup integrity without restore testing.
It requires correct permissions and storage configuration.
Restore and delete actions need human approval before execution.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Recovery Plan
use_cases.description: Create a documented backup and restore plan for important Proxmox workloads.

use_cases.title: Run a Recovery Test
use_cases.description: Plan a controlled restore test and record validation steps for applications.

use_cases.title: Review Backup Compliance
use_cases.description: Summarize backup coverage, retention gaps, and storage usage for audit review.

prompt_templates.title: Check Backup Coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Review backup coverage for my Proxmox VMs and containers. Identify missing backups, old backups, and storage concerns.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Snapshot Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a snapshot plan before updates for these Proxmox workloads. Include rollback steps and validation checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Restore Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a disaster recovery test for critical Proxmox services. Include restore order, test environment needs, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Retention Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Proxmox backup retention strategy with RTO, RPO, storage capacity, compliance needs, and offsite backup requirements.

output_examples.input: Create a backup review for VMs 100, 101, and container 201.
output_examples.output: A backup coverage summary with last backup dates, missing items, storage usage notes, and recommended next actions.

output_examples.input: Plan a restore test for the payroll application.
output_examples.output: A restore test checklist covering backup selection, isolated recovery, application validation, timing, and final documentation.

output_examples.input: Create a disaster recovery report for audit review.
output_examples.output: A professional report summarizing recovery objectives, backup schedules, retention policy, test results, and open risks.

best_practices: Confirm backup targets, retention rules, and restore approvals before running operations.
Test restores regularly in an isolated environment before relying on backups.
Keep recovery documentation current after infrastructure or application changes.
anti_patterns: Restoring production systems without a verified backup and rollback plan.
Deleting old backups before confirming retention and compliance requirements.
Assuming snapshots are a complete substitute for independent backups.

faq.question: Can this skill create Proxmox backups?
faq.answer: It describes workflows for creating VM and container backups through available Proxmox tools.

faq.question: Does this skill verify backup integrity?
faq.answer: It can guide integrity checks and restore tests, but real verification requires testing a restored workload.

faq.question: Can it restore production workloads?
faq.answer: It supports restore workflows, but production restores should require explicit approval and a tested rollback plan.

faq.question: Does it manage backup retention?
faq.answer: It helps plan and review retention policies, storage usage, and cleanup decisions.

faq.question: Is this useful for compliance reporting?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help summarize backup coverage, recovery tests, retention policy, and open continuity risks.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
